invariant_definition: A primary commentary on a *sūtra* or *śāstra*. The *bhāṣya* is the school-defining exegetical work; in Vedānta, every school is constituted by a *bhāṣya* on the *Brahma-Sūtra* (Śaṅkara, Bhāskara, Yādava-Prakāśa, Rāmānuja, Madhva, Nimbārka, Vallabha, Baladeva, Vijñānabhikṣu). A *bhāṣya* is more than a paraphrase — it constructs the doctrinal architecture of the school. Every later genre (*vārttika*, *ṭīkā*, *vivaraṇa*) presupposes and elaborates a *bhāṣya*.

per_school.definition: Each Vedāntic school is constituted by its founder's *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya*. Auxiliary *bhāṣya*s on the *Bhagavad-Gītā* and on the principal *Upaniṣads* (the *prasthāna-traya* commentaries) further fix doctrine.

translator_note: 'Commentary' is acceptable; the precise genre-hierarchy (*bhāṣya* > *vārttika* > *ṭīkā* > *upaṭīkā*, etc.) has no English equivalent and should be preserved.
